name: PyMuPDF
tagline: Fast Python library for extracting and rendering PDF and document content
seoTitle: PyMuPDF - PDF Text & Table Extraction in Python
metaDescription: PyMuPDF is a Python library built on MuPDF for extracting text, tables, and images from PDFs, plus rendering, OCR, and LLM-ready Markdown output.
overview: PyMuPDF is a Python library for reading, extracting, and rendering content from PDFs and other document formats. It is built on top of MuPDF, a small C engine, and exposes both low-level access to document internals and higher-level helper methods. It installs with a single pip command and has no mandatory external dependencies.

It is aimed at developers who need to pull text, tables, images, or layout metadata out of documents, or who need to render, annotate, redact, merge, and split PDFs. Beyond plain text, it can return per-span detail like font, size, color, and bounding boxes, which is useful when document structure matters.

Within parsing and ingestion for RAG pipelines, PyMuPDF handles the step of turning raw PDFs into usable data. The companion pymupdf4llm package converts documents to structure-aware Markdown that you can pass straight to an LLM or a vector store.
features: Text extraction as plain text or a rich dict with font, size, color, and bounding-box metadata
Table detection with find_tables(), exporting to Markdown or a Pandas DataFrame
Page rendering to images at any DPI, plus embedded image extraction
Tesseract-based OCR for scanned pages, with configurable language
Annotation, redaction, form filling, and PDF editing (merge, split, reorder pages)
LLM-ready Markdown and JSON output via the pymupdf4llm extra

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package with pip and open a document to start extracting content. No external dependencies are required for the core library.

steps.heading: Install PyMuPDF
steps.body: Wheels are available for Windows, macOS, and Linux on Python 3.10 to 3.14. If no wheel exists for your platform, pip compiles from source and needs a C/C++ toolchain.

steps.heading: Extract text from a PDF
steps.body: Open the document and read text page by page.
steps.code:
import pymupdf

doc = pymupdf.open("document.pdf")
for page in doc:
    print(page.get_text())

steps.heading: Extract tables
steps.body: Use find_tables() to locate tables and export them as Markdown or a Pandas DataFrame.
steps.code:
import pymupdf

doc = pymupdf.open("spreadsheet.pdf")
page = doc[0]

tables = page.find_tables()
for table in tables:
    print(table.to_markdown())
    df = table.to_pandas()

steps.heading: Convert to Markdown for LLMs
steps.body: Install the pymupdf4llm extra to get structure-aware Markdown you can pass to an LLM or vector store.
steps.code:
pip install pymupdf4llm

# then in Python:
# import pymupdf4llm
# md = pymupdf4llm.to_markdown("report.pdf")

useCases: Ingesting PDFs into a RAG pipeline by converting them to clean Markdown for chunking and embedding
Pulling structured tables out of reports or spreadsheets-as-PDF into Pandas for analysis
Extracting text with layout metadata (font, size, position) when document structure matters
Rendering pages to images, OCRing scanned documents, or redacting sensitive content before sharing
